question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R0 (CE1) removes `network 10.10.0.1/32` from its BGP IPv4 AF. CE1
stops originating the customer prefix toward PE1. PE1's VRF CUSTOMER
BGP table loses 10.10.0.1/32. No VPNv4 advertisement to PE2. R5 (CE2)
stops seeing CE1's network. The eBGP session stays Established; no
syslog event — it's a pure data-plane / prefix-origination failure.
Classic "operator removed a network statement" customer outage.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R0 (CE1), network 10.10.0.1/32 was removed from the BGP IPv4 address-family, so CE1 no longer originates its customer prefix toward PE1.
Because the prefix is never injected into BGP, the eBGP session continues to operate normally yet carries nothing for this route: 10.10.0.1/32 is absent from PE1's VRF CUSTOMER table, is never redistributed as a VPNv4 route to PE2, and therefore cannot reach R5 (CE2) - a pure prefix-origination outage with no session-level breakage.
Fix: re-add network 10.10.0.1/32 under R0's BGP IPv4 unicast AF.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R0: neighbor 10.1.1.0.
- R2: neighbor 10.1.1.1.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R0 (1 instance):
- R0: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65001
address-family ipv4 unicast
 no network 10.10.0.1/32

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65001
address-family ipv4 unicast
 network 10.10.0.1/32

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:21:47.847 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 10.1.1.0 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:21:47.848 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 10.1.1.1 Up (VRF: CUSTOMER) (AS: 65001)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:21:47.848 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
